title: Bhairav Chalisa
intro: Shri Bhairav Chalisa - Hymn to Lord Bhairav
description:
॥ Shri Kaal-Bhairav Chalisa ॥

॥ Doha ॥
Ganpati, guru gauri pad, prem sahit dhari maath.
Chalisa vandan karon, shri shiv bhairavnath.
Shri bhairav sankat haran, mangal karan kripaal.
Shyam varan vikraal vapu, lochan laal vishaal.

॥ Chaupai ॥
Jai jai shri kaali ke laala. Jayati jayati kashi-kutwala.
Jayati batuk bhairav jay haari. Jayati kaal bhairav balkaari.
Jayati sarv bhairav vikhyaata. Jayati naath bhairav sukhdaata.
Bhairav rupa kiyo shiv dhaaran. Bhav ke bhaar utaaran kaaran.
Bhairav rav sun hai bhay doori. Sab vidhi hoy kaamana poori.
Shesh mahesh aadi gun gaayo. Kashi-kotwal kehlaayo.
Jataajut sir chandra birajat. Baala, mukut, bijaayath saajat.
Kati kardhani ghunghru baajat. Darshan karat sakal bhay bhaajat.
Jeevan daan daas ko deenho. Keenho kripa naath tab cheenho.
Vasi rasana bani saarad-kaali. Deenyo var raakhyo mam laali.
Dhanya dhanya bhairav bhay bhanjan. Jay manranjan khal dal bhanjan.
Kar trishool damaru shuchi koda. Kripa kataaksh suyash nahin thoda.
Jo bhairav nirbhay gun gaavat. Ashtasiddhi navnidhi phal paavat.
Rupa vishaal kathin dukh mochan. Krodh karaal laal duhun lochan.
Aganit bhoot pret sang dolat. Bam bam bam shiv bam bam bolat.
Rudrakaya kaali ke laala. Ha kaalahu ke ho kaala.
Batuk naath ho kaal gambheera. Shvet, rakt aru shyam shareera.
Karat teenahu rupa prakaasha. Bharat subhaktan kaham shubh aasha.
Ratna jadit kanchan sinhaasan. Vyaghra charma shuchi narm suaanan.
Tumhi jaayi kaashihin jan dhyavahin. Vishvanath kaham darshan paavahin.
Jay prabhu sanhaarak sunand jay. Jay unnat har umaanand jay.
Bheem trilokan shwaan saath jay. Baijnath shri jagatnath jay.
Mahabheem bheeshan shareer jay. Rudra tryambak dheer veer jay.
Ashvanath jay pretnath jay. Shvaanaarudh saychandra naath jay.
Nimish digambar chakranaath jay. Gahat anaathan naath haath jay.
Treshlesh bhootesh chandra jay. Krodh vats amaresh nand jay.
Shri vaaman nakulesh chand jay. Krityaau keerati prachand jay.
Rudra batuk krodesh kaal dhar. Chakra tund dash paanivyaal dhar.
Kari mad paan shambhu gungaavat. Chaunsath yogin sang nachaavat.
Karat kripa jan par bahu dhanga. Kashi kotwal adabanga.
Deyam kaal bhairav jab sota. Naasai paap mota se mota.
Jaakar nirmal hoy shareera. Mitai sakal sankat bhav peera.
Shri bhairav bhooton ke raaja. Baadha harat karat shubh kaaja.
Ailaadi ke dukh nivaarayo. Sada kripa kari kaaj sammaarayo.
Sundardaas sahit anuraaga. Shri durvaasa nikat prayaaga.
Shri bhairav jee ki jay lekhyo. Sakal kaamana pooran dekhyo.

॥ Doha ॥
Jai jai jai bhairav batuk, swami sankat taar.
Kripa daas par keejiye, shankar ke avtaar.
Jo yeh chalisa padhe, prem sahit sat baar.
Us ghar sarvaanand hon, vaibhav bade apaar.
meaning:
॥ Shri Kaal-Bhairav Chalisa ॥

॥ Doha ॥
Ganpati, guru gauri pad, prem sahit dhari maath.
[With love, bowing my head at the feet of Ganpati, Guru, and Gauri,]
Chalisa vandan karon, shri shiv bhairavnath.
[I offer my worship through this Chalisa to Shri Shiv Bhairavnath.]
Shri bhairav sankat haran, mangal karan kripaal.
[Shri Bhairav is the remover of difficulties, the merciful doer of auspiciousness.]
Shyam varan vikraal vapu, lochan laal vishaal.
[He has a dark complexion, a fearsome body, and large red eyes.]

॥ Chaupai ॥
Jai jai shri kaali ke laala. Jayati jayati kashi-kutwala.
[Victory to the son of Maa Kali; Victory to the Guardian (Kotwal) of Kashi.]
Jayati batuk bhairav jay haari. Jayati kaal bhairav balkaari.
[Victory to Batuk Bhairav, the remover of sorrows; Victory to the powerful Kaal Bhairav.]
Jayati sarv bhairav vikhyaata. Jayati naath bhairav sukhdaata.
[Victory to the famous Bhairav; Victory to Lord Bhairav, the giver of happiness.]
Bhairav rupa kiyo shiv dhaaran. Bhav ke bhaar utaaran kaaran.
[Lord Shiva took the form of Bhairav to remove the burden of the world.]
Bhairav rav sun hai bhay doori. Sab vidhi hoy kaamana poori.
[Hearing the roar of Bhairav removes fear and fulfills all desires.]
Shesh mahesh aadi gun gaayo. Kashi-kotwal kehlaayo.
[Shesh, Mahesh, and others sing your praises; you are called the Kotwal of Kashi.]
Jataajut sir chandra birajat. Baala, mukut, bijaayath saajat.
[Your head has matted hair with the moon, adorned with a crown and ornaments.]
Kati kardhani ghunghru baajat. Darshan karat sakal bhay bhaajat.
[Ghunghrus ring on your waist; seeing you destroys all fear.]
Jeevan daan daas ko deenho. Keenho kripa naath tab cheenho.
[You gave the gift of life to your devotee; I recognize your grace, O Lord.]
Vasi rasana bani saarad-kaali. Deenyo var raakhyo mam laali.
[Saraswati and Kali reside on my tongue; you granted a boon and protected me.]
Dhanya dhanya bhairav bhay bhanjan. Jay manranjan khal dal bhanjan.
[Glory to Bhairav, destroyer of fear; Victory to the one who pleases the mind and destroys the wicked.]
Kar trishool damaru shuchi koda. Kripa kataaksh suyash nahin thoda.
[You hold the trident, damaru, and a whip; your glance is gracious and your fame is immense.]
Jo bhairav nirbhay gun gaavat. Ashtasiddhi navnidhi phal paavat.
[He who sings the praises of Bhairav fearlessly obtains the eight siddhis and nine nidhis.]
Rupa vishaal kathin dukh mochan. Krodh karaal laal duhun lochan.
[Your form is immense, you free one from difficult sorrows; your eyes are red with rage.]
Aganit bhoot pret sang dolat. Bam bam bam shiv bam bam bolat.
[Countless ghosts and spirits roam with you; chanting 'Bam Bam' to Shiva.]
Rudrakaya kaali ke laala. Ha kaalahu ke ho kaala.
[You are Rudra-bodied, son of Kali; you are the death of Death itself.]
Batuk naath ho kaal gambheera. Shvet, rakt aru shyam shareera.
[You are Batuk Nath, the grave Kaal; with white, red, and dark bodies.]
Karat teenahu rupa prakaasha. Bharat subhaktan kaham shubh aasha.
[You manifest in these three forms, giving hope to the virtuous.]
Ratna jadit kanchan sinhaasan. Vyaghra charma shuchi narm suaanan.
[Seated on a gem-studded golden throne, on a tiger skin, with a gentle face.]
Tumhi jaayi kaashihin jan dhyavahin. Vishvanath kaham darshan paavahin.
[Those who meditate on you in Kashi get the vision of Vishwanath.]
Jay prabhu sanhaarak sunand jay. Jay unnat har umaanand jay.
[Victory to the Lord of destruction, victory to Sunanda; Victory to the exalted Har, victory to Umananda.]
Bheem trilokan shwaan saath jay. Baijnath shri jagatnath jay.
[Victory to Bheem of the three worlds, accompanied by a dog; Victory to Baijnath, Lord of the world.]
Mahabheem bheeshan shareer jay. Rudra tryambak dheer veer jay.
[Victory to the immensely terrifying one; Victory to Rudra Tryambak, the steady warrior.]
Ashvanath jay pretnath jay. Shvaanaarudh saychandra naath jay.
[Victory to Ashvanath, victory to Pretnath; Victory to the dog-rider, the moon-crowned Lord.]
Nimish digambar chakranaath jay. Gahat anaathan naath haath jay.
[Victory to the momentary one, Digambara, Chakranath; Victory to the Lord who holds the hands of the helpless.]
Treshlesh bhootesh chandra jay. Krodh vats amaresh nand jay.
[Victory to Treshlesh, Bhutesh, Chandra; Victory to the anger-controller, son of Amaresh.]
Shri vaaman nakulesh chand jay. Krityaau keerati prachand jay.
[Victory to Vaman, Nakulesh, Chand; Victory to the one of immense glory.]
Rudra batuk krodesh kaal dhar. Chakra tund dash paanivyaal dhar.
[Rudra Batuk, Lord of anger, holder of Time; Chakra-Tund, holder of ten serpents.]
Kari mad paan shambhu gungaavat. Chaunsath yogin sang nachaavat.
[Drinking nectar, you sing Shambhu's praises and make the 64 Yoginis dance.]
Karat kripa jan par bahu dhanga. Kashi kotwal adabanga.
[You shower grace in many ways, O unique Kotwal of Kashi.]
Deyam kaal bhairav jab sota. Naasai paap mota se mota.
[When Kaal Bhairav strikes with his whip, it destroys even the greatest sins.]
Jaakar nirmal hoy shareera. Mitai sakal sankat bhav peera.
[By this, the body becomes pure, and all worldly pains and troubles vanish.]
Shri bhairav bhooton ke raaja. Baadha harat karat shubh kaaja.
[Shri Bhairav is the King of ghosts; he removes obstacles and does auspicious works.]
Ailaadi ke dukh nivaarayo. Sada kripa kari kaaj sammaarayo.
[You removed Ailaadi's sorrows and always protected him with grace.]
Sundardaas sahit anuraaga. Shri durvaasa nikat prayaaga.
[Sundardas, with love, near Prayag and Durvasa wrote this.]
Shri bhairav jee ki jay lekhyo. Sakal kaamana pooran dekhyo.
[He who writes/reads this victory of Shri Bhairav Ji, sees all his desires fulfilled.]

॥ Doha ॥
Jai jai jai bhairav batuk, swami sankat taar.
[Victory to Bhairav Batuk, O Lord, remove my troubles.]
Kripa daas par keejiye, shankar ke avtaar.
[Bestow your grace on your devotee, O Avatar of Shankar.]
Jo yeh chalisa padhe, prem sahit sat baar.
[He who reads this Chalisa with love a hundred times.]
Us ghar sarvaanand hon, vaibhav bade apaar.
[That house shall have all happiness and immense wealth.]

faq.q1: Why is the Bhairav Chalisa recited?
faq.a1: Lord Bhairav is considered a 'Protector.' This Chalisa is recited to seek protection from negative energies, evil eyes, obstacles created by enemies, and mental fears.
faq.q2: Should it be read at night?
faq.a2: Yes, Kaal Bhairav is considered the deity of the night. Worshiping him and reciting his Chalisa during the night or after dusk is considered extremely powerful and fruitful.
faq.q3: Does reading the Bhairav Chalisa create fear?
faq.a3: Absolutely not. Bhairav Ji is the protector of his devotees. Reciting the Chalisa removes unknown fears and anxieties from the mind; it does not increase them.
faq.q4: What is the best day for this?
faq.a4: Sunday and Tuesday are considered the best days for the worship of Kaal Bhairav.
faq.q5: Does it require any special tantra or complex rituals?
faq.a5: No, Lord Bhairav craves sincere devotion. You do not need any complex rituals; you can recite it with full faith and purity.
